Solar Panels (70)

Battery Storage (15)

Instyle Solar News (12)

Blog > Battery Storage

Tesla’s Powerwall 2 solar battery is a high-performing and reliable solution for residential and light commercial applications. The energy stored in this battery helps maintain your electricity supply during blackouts and peak usage times. And with the intuitive Tesla mobile app, you’ll always be able to monitor your energy consumption.

The battery unit is an essential part of your solar PV system. It enables you to have grid independence and financial savings. With a reliable solar battery, you can power your home or business entirely through your solar panels’ clean energy.

When it comes to leading solar batteries, one particular brand is always in the discussion. As such, our review highlights everything you need to know about the Tesla Powerwall 2 solar battery and how it compares to other options in the market.

Read on to explore the Powerwall 2 features and specifications.

​

What is the Tesla Powerwall 2?

Source

The Tesla Powerwall 2 is a fully integrated lithium-ion battery system for households and small businesses. It delivers energy storage to power your house during an outage, peak usage times, and at night. This AC battery is an ideal solution for self-consumption, time-based control, and backup.

In 2015, Tesla released the first Powerwall, and its current iteration entered the market in 2016. Its AC battery allows for easy retrofitting to an existing solar system for energy storage outside sunlight hours. Only one model is currently available, but you can stack up multiple solar batteries for increased storage. 

Your home or facility will have a simple connection with the Powerwall’s electrical interface. The battery storage unit has a compact design which enables swift installation. With this market-leading energy density, you’ll quickly enjoy the benefits of solar power.

The Powerwall 2 recharges via solar panels or through the grid during off-peak hours to help you save on your electricity bills. Whenever there’s an outage, your panels will continue charging the Powerwall. You’ll no longer need to rely on bulky, expensive battery banks that drain excess solar power from your panels.

Is the Tesla Powerwall 2 Solar Battery Worth it?

The Tesla Powerwall 2 is undoubtedly helpful for those looking to reduce their energy expenses. In fact, this battery can eliminate your electricity bills altogether. Mid to large-sized households can potentially save thousands of dollars per month with it.

With Powerwall 2, you can produce excess energy through your solar panel system and feed it back to the electricity grid to earn feed-in tariffs. It also allows for energy independence as you won’t have to rely as much on grid power. This utility is great for people living in areas that experience frequent blackouts.

Pairing solar with your battery storage means you’ll also maintain a minimal carbon footprint by storing clean energy from your solar panels.

The Tesla Powerwall 2 charges with any electricity source and is an excellent way to save on electricity costs. It’s a lithium-ion home battery that can store DC energy from your photovoltaic panels.

​

How Much Storage does the Tesla Powerwall 2 have?

The Powerwall 2 has 13.5 kWh of solar battery storage. The solar battery can fully charge in 2 hours with a 6 kW solar system in perfect condition and free of loads.

With a solar power output reaching 5 kW, this battery can meet all your energy needs, even at peak hours. You can have multiple appliances running free of concern.

​

How Long Will the Tesla Powerwall 2 Power My Home?

The average household consumes 28 kWh per day, and a Powerwall has 13.5 kWh capacity of energy storage with a discharge rate of 5 kW.

Based on this, a Tesla battery could power your home for 12 hours straight. It can generate 50% of your home’s required power for the entire evening period.

With two Powerwall units, you can expect 18 hours of power supply. Three Powerwall units will keep your home running for 24 hours. So you have complete flexibility when it comes to measuring your off-grid ratio to traditional electricity supply.

​

Tesla Powerwall 2 Battery Technical Specifications

The Powerwall 2 is a DC electricity storage system with an original capacity of 13.5 kWh per Powerwall. You must purchase more Powerwalls in parallel for increased solar battery storage capacity. This way, you’ll have more stored power in the battery and be more prepared for an outage.

Source

Below are the technical specifications for the Tesla Powerwall 2 battery. More information is available in the unit’s datasheet:

Performance SpecificationsTesla Powerwall 2
AC Voltage (Nominal)230 V
Feed-In TypeSingle Phase
Grid Frequency50 Hz
Total Energy14 kWh
Usable Energy13.5 kWh
Real Power (Continuous)5 kW (charge and discharge)
Apparent Power (Continuous)5 kVA (charge and discharge)
Maximum Supply Fault Current10 kA
Maximum Output Fault Current32 A
Power Factor Output Range+/– 1.0 adjustable
Internal Battery DC Voltage50 V
Round Trip Efficiency90%
Warranty10 years
Environmental SpecificationsTesla Powerwall 2
Operating Temperature–20 °C to 50 °C
Recommended Temperature0 °C to 30 °C
Operating Humidity (RH)Up to 100% while condensing
Storage Conditions–20 °C to 30 °C (up to 95% RH non-condensing),
State of Energy (SoE): 25% initial
Maximum Elevation3,000 m
EnvironmentIndoor and outdoor rated
IngressRating IP67 (Battery & Power Electronics),
IP56 (Wiring Compartment)
Wet Location RatingYes
Noise Level @ 1 m< 40 dBA at 30 °C
Mechanical SpecificationsTesla Powerwall 2
Dimensions1,150 mm x 753 mm x 147 mm 
Weight114 kg 
Mounting OptionsFloor or wall mount 

​

Tesla Powerwall 2 Battery Key Features

The Tesla Powerwall 2 features an app where you can choose between three different battery modes. It operates at a great temperature range between -4 to +122 °F. Other key features include battery stack up, a built-in AC inverter, and more.

The Tesla Powerwall includes a built-in AC inverter, making it extremely easy to install. 

  • Its AC-coupled battery will continue operating even when your solar inverter malfunctions. If your battery also experiences a fault, the Powerwall 2 allows your photovoltaic panels to continue supplying electricity to your home through their own inverter.
  • The Tesla Powerwall 2 has a liquid cooling system. It is the only residential battery system on the market with such technology. This feature allows the battery to work at a temperature range of -4 to +122 °F.
  • Unlike many lithium solar batteries, the Tesla Powerwall 2 doesn’t reduce its power output at ambient temperatures above 104 °F. The unit can disperse more heat under high loads thanks to its liquid cooling ability.
  • It has a thermal management system for the battery to charge normally at temperatures below freezing point. Other lithium solar batteries suffer from significantly reduced charge rates in this situation.
  • The Powerwall 2 preheats the lithium-ion batteries at low temperatures, enabling them to charge at a standard rate. Tesla solar batteries use grid power to preheat the lithium cells, but during off-grid operations or a blackout, they use some residual energy to achieve that.
  • Tesla’s Powerwall 2 comes with a user-friendly app for you to monitor and control your electricity consumption. With its Power Flow screen, you can see exactly where your stored energy is coming from and where it goes.

Source

This app allows you to choose between three battery operation modes:

1. Self-powered mode

This mode allows you to use stored energy to power your home after sunset. You can also select the percentage to reserve for unexpected outages. 

2. Backup-only mode

This mode reserves 100% of your Powerwall for backup in case of an outage.

3. Advanced mode

In this mode, you set up time-based control to save costs on electricity consumption. For example, you can set your Powerwall to avoid charges from the grid at peak hours to maximise savings, as energy is most costly from 5 to 8 pm (Peak hours).

Another inclusion is the Storm Watch function. This feature allows the app to communicate with the National Weather Service. It will collect data from the service and update you if bad weather is imminent in your area.

The Storm Watch will charge your Powerwall 2 for peak power output. A blackout won’t have any significant impact on your daily life, as the battery can provide reliable backup power.

​

Is the Tesla Powerwall 2 Easy to Install?

Source

You should first choose the optimal location to install the Tesla Powerwall and determine if you want it mounted on the wall or the floor. The correct site provides adequate protection for the device. It’s advisable to choose a location close to the solar panel.

With the Powerwall 2, you have installation flexibility as it’s water-resistant, touch-safe, and dust-proof. If you live in a region with extreme temperatures, installing the Powerwall 2 inside your garage would be the best option.

Be sure to choose an expert installer to avoid issues such as wiring, local code, or even flooding. You may need special transportation equipment to move the Powerwall 2 to your desired location.

Depending on the solar battery’s usage case, you might also require additional equipment. For example, if your Powerwall 2 serves as a backup electricity source for essential loads, you must have a new sub-panel installed. This connects the unit to the circuits in your house that will back up during a power outage.

You can achieve 100% home backup wastage with the Powerwall 2, but this is only possible based on your home’s electrical load and usage patterns. 

​

Is the Tesla Powerwall 2 Battery Reliable? Does it Include a Warranty?

Source

The Tesla Powerwall 2 battery is a dependable solution during power outages. With a capacity of 13.5 kilowatt-hours, you’ll always have energy reserves at peak times and at night.

Thanks to the battery stack-up feature, you can combine several Powerwalls for more usage capacity. By stacking up two Tesla units, you can create your off-grid power system.

Powerwall 2 systems can provide 5.8 kW of continuous power on sunny days. Your Tesla home solar battery can produce enough energy for electrical power outlets and most 120-volt appliances.

Expect an output between 7 kW and 9.6 kW in a power outage. The exact amount depends on the weather conditions. 

The Powerwall 2 is just as dependable as other Tesla products. It comes with a 10-year product warranty for peace of mind.

​

Is the Tesla Powerwall 2 Solar Battery Safe?

Safety is key when constructing the Tesla Powerwall 2, and the company is willing to put its battery system to the test. Designed to meet or exceed the most stringent safety standards, the Powerwall 2 won’t pose any fire hazard provided that you follow Tesla’s installation requirements.

Tests carried out by Tesla show that if a fire emits from the pod, it won’t spread to the rest of the Powerwall. Likewise, the surrounding batteries will be unaffected if there is a fire around one system.

Advantages

The Powerwall 2 is one of the most capable Tesla products on the market today. It has superior solar power storage to many other competitors. Expect a battery life of 12 – 15 hours for powering a few lights, the refrigerator, and your TV.

However, energy levels will deplete rapidly when using your dishwasher or HVAC. In this case, it will last for about 4 to 5 hours. While you may think this isn’t sufficient to last through a blackout, there’s a solution to this problem.

Thanks to the Powerwall batteries stacking up, you can outlast any power outage. Just tell your installer to connect additional units for increased capacity. 

Other advantages of using the Tesla Powerwall 2 include:

  • The biggest battery capacity available
  • Solid performance at low and high temperatures
  • Easy maintenance
  • Three energy consumption modes
  • Excellent round-trip efficiency
  • Ability to stack up to ten batteries
  • More off-grid power generation
  • 10-year warranty.

Disadvantages

The only major shortcoming of the Powerwall 2 is that it needs an additional inverter. This may not be an issue for those who already have a system. However, it could turn off potential customers.

Unlike other technologies, the Powerwall 2 battery package doesn’t include an inverter. You’ll have to opt for the Tesla Powerwall+ battery if you want an integrated solar inverter.

At least two Powerwall batteries are required to go completely off-grid. This can be disappointing if you want to disconnect from the grid entirely with just one system. You should consider this when looking for an off-grid energy supply. It’s also an expensive unit compared to similar solutions on the market.

With that said, the Tesla Powerwall 2 is an excellent choice for consumers with the budget to accommodate purchasing two Tesla batteries.

​

Which Inverters are Compatible with the Tesla Powerwall 2 Battery?

The Tesla Powerwall 2 battery has numerous options for solar inverter compatibility. You can opt for Fronius, SMA, SolarEdge, Enphase Microinverter, ABB, or Delta and integrate it with your existing solar panel system. The company is constantly exploring compatibility with different inverter manufacturers.

The Powerwall 2 is an AC battery, so it requires a separate solar inverter to function with a solar array. All current solar inverters mesh well with the Tesla Powerwall. If there’s a blackout, the battery continues to work in what is called island mode.

This might be a problem if you don’t have a solar inverter that links directly to the firewall. In this case, your battery won’t recharge from your solar PV system when there’s a blackout. The entire system will be inactive until the grid is back online.

Instead, we recommend using solar inverters from top manufacturers like Enphase and SMA. Contact our solar experts if you’re unsure what brand to choose. They’ll provide a solution for your solar needs.

​

Next Steps…

Interested in solar? By clicking below, you can use our smart solar calculator to find out just how much you could save with solar, what rebate you are eligible for, and your impact on the environment.

Don’t wait until next quarter’s bloated bill, and get started today!

Otherwise, you can always contact us on 1300 133 556 or email us at sales@instylesolar.com.au.

​

Tehbyn Nova

HVLP PAINT SPRAYER

Equalizer APO / Documentation Wiki / Documentation

Web Clip

  • Home
  • / Browse
  • / Equalizer APO
  • / Documentation Wiki

Equalizer APO Documentation Wiki

A system-wide equalizer for Windows 7 / 8 / 8.1 / 10 / 11

Brought to you by: jthedering
  • Summary
  • Files
  • Reviews
  • Support
  • Downloads
  • Documentation Wiki
  • Code
  • Tickets
  • Discussion

  • Wiki Home
  • Browse Pages
  • Browse Labels
  • Formatting Help

Documentation   

Authors:

Welcome to the Wiki of Equalizer APO. This is the documentation for users of Equalizer APO. Developers might also be interested in reading the developer documentation. To begin using Equalizer APO, you should read the tutorials. After that, you can look at more detailed information in the configuration reference.

Table of contents:

  • Installation tutorial
  • Configuration tutorial
  • Configuration file format
  • Troubleshooting
    • Configurator
    • Control Panel
    • Log files
    • Hardware-accelerated OpenAL

Installation tutorial

  1. Download the Equalizer APO setup for your version of Windows (32 or 64 bit). If you are unsure if you need the 32 or the 64 bit version, you can open Start Menu -> Control Panel -> System and look up the system type.
  2. Execute the setup program and follow the instructions. Remember your installation path if you don't use the default of C:Program FilesEqualizerAPO . From here on, for better readability it is assumed that you use the default path.
  3. During the installation process the program Configurator.exe will be run. Make sure that you select the correct audio device to install the APO to. If you are unsure you can open Start Menu -> Control Panel -> Sound and look for the default output device. If you need to install the APO to other audio devices later, you can run the program again from C:Program FilesEqualizerAPOConfigurator.exe .
  4. After the installation finished, you should allow the reboot of your system. This is needed because the newly installed APO will not be used immediately but only after the audio service is restarted.
  5. When the system has rebooted, the APO should be active. This will only be noticable by a small reduction in volume and a mild low frequency boost, because this is what the example configuration file specifies. To change the APO's behaviour to something more useful, you can now read the Configuration chapter.

Configuration tutorial

  1. Open Windows Explorer and navigate to C:Program FilesEqualizerAPOconfig . You should find the files config.txt and example.txt. The file config.txt is the main configuration file that will automatically be loaded by Equalizer APO.
  2. Open the file config.txt in a text editor and you will see that it first defines a preamplification value and then includes example.txt. To check if the APO is really working you can start some audio or video application and adjust the preamp value while music is running. You should notice that the volume changes immediately each time after you save the file.
  3. To begin creating your individual filter configuration you should now install and run Room EQ Wizard.

Screenshot of Room EQ Wizard (click to enlarge):

A detailed explanation of the usage of Room EQ Wizard is out of the scope of this document, but here is the basic process:

  1. Click the "Measure" button in the toolbar (Mark A in the above screenshot) to bring up the measurement dialog. Here you should first do "Check Levels" and adjust your output volume appropriately, then "Start Measuring". After the measurement, the dialog will close automatically and a frequency response graph is shown.
  2. Click the "EQ" button (Mark B) in the toolbar. Here you can select an equalizer type (Mark C). Use either "Generic" or, if you prefer bandwidth instead of Q, the "FBQ2496" equalizer type. Beware that no other equalizer types are currently supported.
  3. Click the "EQ Filters" button (Mark D) in the EQ window. Now you can add filters by setting "Control" to Manual, "Type" to PK/PEQ and then adjusting "Frequency", "Gain" and "Q"/"Bw Oct" to your needs. The graph in the EQ window will directly show the filter's frequency response. Since version 0.8, you can also use any of the other filter types available if they suit your needs, but generally the peaking filters should be the most appropriate for room correction.
  4. To save the filter settings, you should first use the "Save this filter set" button (Mark E) in the EQ Filters window. This will save the settings in a format that Room EQ Wizard can read back later when you need to make further adjustments.
  5. Now save the filter settings in a format that Equalizer APO can read. To do this, go to the main window of Room EQ Wizard. Open the "File" menu (Mark F) and select "Export" -> "Filter Settings as text". Save under a new filename into C:Program FilesEqualizerAPOconfig .
  6. Open C:Program FilesEqualizerAPOconfigconfig.txt in a text editor and change the "Include" line to refer to your newly created configuration file. The change should be effective immediately.

Congratulations, you have now created your first configuration for Equalizer APO. To learn more about the usage of RoomEQWizard, you can look into its help file. The process can even be automated to some extent, as is explained in this forum thread.

Configuration file format

This information has been moved to the configuration reference.

Troubleshooting

This section describes approaches to solve possible problems impeding the successful operation of Equalizer APO.

Configurator

By default, Equalizer APO will try to keep the functionality of other APOs that have been shipped with the sound card driver ("original APOs"). In some cases, this causes instabilities in the audio processing. The Configurator offers troubleshooting options to adjust how the original APOs are used.

If you experience instabilities during playback or recording when using Equalizer APO, you can try to disable the usage of the original APOs in the Configurator:
1. Select your audio device by clicking on its connection name.
2. Enable the troubleshooting options.
3. Uncheck both "Use original APO" checkboxes.
(click to enlarge)
Note that you will lose all features that the sound card driver realizes through its APOs. You can also try to uncheck only one of the check boxes to preserve some functionality.

Some sound card drivers disable options when they detect that another APO has been registered. You can uncheck one of the "Install APO" checkboxes to only install Equalizer APO in the pre-mix or post-mix stage. For the other stage, the original APO will be registered then, which may help to recover some options of the sound card driver.

Control Panel

If you installed Equalizer APO and no changes to the configuration file lead to any changes in the signal, APOs might have been disabled for the device in the Control Panel.
To check this, open Start Menu -> Control Panel -> Sound and double click on your audio device to open the properties dialog.

If the dialog has an "Enhancements" tab, go to that tab. You should see a view similar to the left screenshot below. Make sure the "Disable all enhancements" check box (red box) is unchecked, even if you don't use any of the enhancements in the list.

If the dialog does not have an "Enhancements" tab, go to the "Advanced" tab. You should see a view similar to the right screenshot below. Make sure the "Enable audio enhancements" check box (red box) is checked.

Log files

When Equalizer APO encounters a critical problem while running, it writes a line into the log file C:WindowsServiceProfilesLocalServiceAppDataLocalTempEqualizerAPO.log . So, in case of problems this file might contain useful information. Under normal circumstances, this file does not even exist, as it will only be created when an error occurs.

To get more information, you can enable trace messages, which means that Equalizer APO will write lines marked with "(TRACE)" to the file even when running normally. To do this, open regedit.exe, go to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINESOFTWAREEqualizerAPO and set the value EnableTrace to true. Then, when playing back or recording audio via a device that Equalizer APO is installed to, information about initialization and the configuration files will be output to the log file. This might help e.g. to see if the configuration files are interpreted as intended. After you have finished, you should set EnableTrace back to false, so that the log file does not grow unnecessarily.

Hardware-accelerated OpenAL

Normally, applications utilizing OpenAL for their audio output do not present a problem as they will often use DirectSound as their backend, which supports APOs. Some sound card manufacturers however provide OpenAL libraries with hardware-acceleration that access the hardware directly, circumventing APOs. There is no way to enable APO support for hardware-accelerated OpenAL, so the only solution for this is to either switch to another output library, if the application supports that, or to make OpenAL fall back to software.

To force OpenAL to fall back to software, the OpenAL32.dll may be replaced with a different one, for example from http://kcat.strangesoft.net/openal.html
A way to globally disable OpenAL hardware-acceleration however is to move or rename the vendor-specific OpenAL library in C:WindowsSystem32 or C:WindowsSysWOW64, which is often called like *_oal.dll, for example ct_oal.dll . Warning: This is a modification to the sound driver, which is of course not officially supported and can lead to unexpected results.

Discussion

1 2 > >> (Page 1 of 2)

  • a
    John Accardi - 2014-07-19
    

    Hello ... just installed Equalizer APO and reading the documentation it looks like this is just to adjust effects for individual channels .. L, R, Center, etc. I need to be able to boost volume, across all channels (master adjustments), but only for certain frequencies ranges. For example, lower volume in base but boost volume in some frequency ranges. Is this possible with Equalizer APO. If not, any suggestions for Windows applications that do this? Thanks.

     

    • a
      Jonas Thedering - 2014-07-20
      

      The "effects" currently implemented are actually mostly used to adjust the frequency response (the volume) in certain frequency ranges. For example, the peaking EQ filter (PK) takes a center frequency and a bandwidth argument to select the frequency range and its gain argument determines the boost/cut to the volume in that range. Also helpful for you can be the shelving EQ filters (LS/HS) which adjust the volume below/above a given frequency in another way than is possible with peaking filters. Please use Room EQ Wizard as described in the tutorial above, it will show you how the different types of filters adjust the volume at certain frequencies and will make it much easier for you to do the adjustments wanted. You can also use it without doing any measurements, just open REW and go to EQ->EQ Filters.

      Normally, the filters are just applied to all channels, so for your desired behaviour, just don't use the Channel command, which would allow you to limit the filtering to selected channels.

      Also, be careful when using filters with positive gain to boost frequency ranges. If you use these, you should also use the Preamp command to lower the overall volume, so that the output is not clipped.

       

      Last edit: Jonas Thedering 2014-07-20

    THANK YOU!

    Will you be including the ability to resize the EQ window within Configuration Editor? Cause as it is now, I can only zoom in and out and use scroll bars ... makes it difficult to make quick changes.

    • a
      Jonas Thedering - 2015-11-01

  • a
    Tracy Lee - 2015-11-22
    

    As stated before this software is truly amazing! The level of control is unbelievable! Thank you so much.

    It's effects are simply amazing for playing locally stored music! Thanks again.

     

    Last edit: Tracy Lee 2015-11-22

    • a
      Jonas Thedering - 2015-11-22
      

      Maybe you are mixing up two things:
      1. E-APO can only process uncompressed audio data. Therefore, if you have a Dolby Digital bitstream that should be output directly without decoding, E-APO can not process the audio. You would need to set your software to decode the audio so that E-APO can access the uncompressed data. I know that this can be a problem, especially when using SPDIF, where only Dolby Digital Live could be used to recompress the audio after the processing by E-APO so that all channels can still be output via SPDIF. With HDMI this is less of a problem as it has the bandwidth to output multichannel uncompressed audio.
      2. The DisableProtectedAudioDG registry entry is required by E-APO because it is an unsigned APO. Normally, APOs are supplied by the sound card driver and they are signed by WHQL, but E-APO will never get such a signature. This means that the protected audio path is broken, making some software using DRM refuse to output any sound (have seen this with the Music app when trying to play protected music). So if your software checks for the validity of the protected audio path, it is practically incompatible with E-APO.

  • a
    Adam Mc - 2016-07-08
    

    Sorry if this has already been answered, but can I adjust the eq of my mic - i.e. reduce the treble so my voice isn't tinny when I speak to my friends on skype?
    Thanks!

     
    1

  • a
    Peter Verbeek - 2016-07-09
    

    Yes, you can. Select your mic device and equalize it. This can be done in the Configuration Editor or Peace GUI.

     

    • a
      Adam Mc - 2016-07-09
      

      Thank you :)

       

      • a
        Glenn Odagawa - 2016-07-09
        

        "Treble" is such a vague, nondescript word. "Tinny" even worse. From your
        description, it may seem that the reason your voice is "tinny" is a peak in
        the upper-midrange (2.5-6.3Khz region) either in your microphone or your
        friend's playback system, not in the high frequency area (see attached
        article on High Frequency Energy). Try inserting this line in your
        configuration file:

        Filter (): ON PK Fc 3000 Hz Gain -4 dB
        Q 1
        Filter (        ): ON PK Fc 125 Hz Gain +3 dB
        Q 0.8

        * Your next available filter #, no parenthesis, after the last line in the
        file, no blank lines inbetween.

        You may want to use both or one or the other of these filters. You will
        have to tweak the parameters accordingly for you and your friend's audio
        environment.

        Also take into consideration that your mic or your friend's playback system
        may be defective and not performing to spec.

        On Sat, Jul 9, 2016 at 3:57 AM, Adam Mc uktab@users.sf.net wrote:

        Thank you :)

        Sent from sourceforge.net because you indicated interest in
        https://sourceforge.net/p/equalizerapo/wiki/Documentation/

        To unsubscribe from further messages, please visit
        https://sourceforge.net/auth/subscriptions/

        --

        Glenn S. Odagawa

        Chicago, IL(872) 235-7783

         

        • a
          Adam Mc - 2016-07-09
          

          Thanks Glenn. I have a new Skype headset and friends said straight away the sound was more tinny. I've been in the music business for about 30 years, and I'm used to speaking in non-tech terms as many new recording artists don't have any idea about frequencies - hence I talk about bass, mid and treble when explaining things generally as most of the people I work with understand what happens when they move the bass and treble controls on whatever system they use to play their recordings back.
          I appreciate the info on the filters too.
          Adam

    Hi, I hope I'm the right section, I just installed Equalizer apo 32 bit, I don't see the configeration editer I've installed it twice still the same thing. I am running win 7, on an older abit motherboard. What is the problem. Thank you in advance

     

    • a
      Peter Verbeek - 2016-09-14
      

      After installing Equalizer APO there should be Equalizer APO 1.1.2 item in the Windows start menu. Within this folder you can start the Configuration Editor. I think there isn't a desktop shortcut to it.

       

  • a
    Robert Steel - 2016-10-27
    

    Hi,

    I am wandering, How do i Change config.exe BACK to example.exe???

    I did the change and it says that there is somthing wrong with it and i tried reinstalling it but it didn't work.

    Please Help!

    Regards
    Robert

     

    Last edit: Robert Steel 2016-10-27

    • a
      Glenn Odagawa - 2016-10-27
      

      There is no [config.exe] or [example.exe] in EqualizerAPO. There is
      [config.txt] and [example.txt]. If you edit the [(dirve):Program
      FilesEqualizerAPOconfig.txt]       file with Notepad or any other text editor
      it should read something like this:

      Common preamp

      Preamp: +0 dB

      Channel: L
      Preamp: +0 dB
      Include: example.txt

      Channel: R
      Preamp: +0 dB
      Include: example.txt

      If you have modified your [config.txt] file, I've attached the default file
      to this message.

      Regards,

      -Glenn

      On Wed, Oct 26, 2016 at 10:11 PM, Robert Steel cwl900@users.sf.net wrote:

      Hi,

      I am wandering, How do i Change config.exe BACK to example.exe???

      I did the change and it says that there is somthing wrong with it and i
      tried reinstalling it but it didn't work.

      Regards
      Robert

1 2 > >> (Page 1 of 2)

IMG_1551.jpg



IMG_1548.jpg



Pillow List

_________________________________________________________________
Research

Pegasos Swiss Association 

Swiss Law & Requirements

There are several important factors that frame assisted dying in Switzerland.
    1. The Swiss Penal Code allows for assisted suicide, as long as the motive of those assisting is not selfish.
    2. Those assisting the person to die need not be a doctor. Anyone can assist as long as their motive is altruistic.
Furthermore under Swiss law, the person requesting the assistance to die must possess:
    1. decision-making capacity; and
    2. they must have ‘control’ or ‘ownership of the action’ over their death (‘Tatherrschaft’ in German). (See Art. 115 StGB of the Swiss Penal Code & the decision of the Swiss Federal Court BGE 133 I 58).

__________________________________________________________________
Pillow List (second draft)
Second draft: there was a first draft but it became more of a wishful-thinking exercise about what I hope will happen and, more importantly, what I am afraid might happen.  But that is not what I really wanted to convey here. This document is intented to say two things:
  • I love you, if you are reading this, and
  • You have been the very best part of my life—family
  • I want my passing to be as stress- and pain-free as possible for you.
I was going to explain, very carefully, what the triggering tests should be. But now I am reminded that you all have heard them from me before.  I don’t have to explain.
BTW, one of things I have loved about our family is that we kept secrets to a minimum. I know that you all probably held secrets close to your chest—as we all do for a myriad of reasons. But I am confident that we shared almost as much as we could.
  • Unless Terese wants to do it, Hilary, take over the task of calling/text/emailing people with the news.
__________________________________________________________________
Pillow List (first draft)
This document is intended to be the "official" explanation of Thomas's wishes with respect to the question of ending his life because the quality of that life has become so poor that it would be better to just switch off, bringing death perhaps somewhat sooner than the established powers that be would have him die. That is to say, if any of these conditions become true, Thomas would like to die rather than continue living under those conditions.

I, Thomas Robert Brennan-Marquez, am composing this list on 16 Apr 2024 while I am quite healthy in both mind and body. My sincere hope is that this will help anyone who might be wondering if it is really time for me to die. Trying to get ahead of that inevitable day's arrival, I am trying to lay down some conditions that I know, at least from my perspective today, are awful enough that I don't want to live any longer. Having said that, I do want to make clear that I completely trust my immediate family members (Terese Brennan-Marquez , Kiel Brennan-Marquez , and Hilary Brennan) to decide when the time has come. They know my feelings about my end of life so I am comfortable leaving the ultimate decision to them, if I am not able to make that decision for myself.

One more thing I want to make clear: as of this date, 16 Apr 2024, there are only a few legal ways to end life intentionally and I don't know how that situation may change in the future--but what I do not want is for anyone to run the risk of running afoul of the law to end my life.

These are the conditions I wanted to list. All of these descriptions presuppose that the condition will likely not improve. The subjective decision about the odds of improvement I will leave to my immediate family. I sincerely hope I have provided enough detail that the decision will, if/when the time comes, be relatively easy to make. And please know that I appreciate how hard this may be on family members but I also really appreciate that you are trying to do the right thing.
  1. If I don't know who the people in my family are (let's say if that means even half of the family members) as much as 50% of the time, I won't really be around to realize there is family around. In this situation I think it will be just fine for me to die. If the family wants to keep me around for their own reasons (and assuming that "keeping me around" isn't a huge burden on anyone), that will be okay too--but that choice would be my second choice.
  2. If I have become feeble enough, ill enough, or disabled enough that the only reasonable option left is to put me in a nursing home, I don't want to keep living any longer. Nursing home here means a facility that specializes in handling many/all of the chores associated with staying alive: eating, urinating, defecating, breathing, etc. I am confident that I do not wish to have these chores handled by any paid service or facility. Indeed, in the case that one or more family members feel a desire/responsibility to handle those chores for me, my preference would be that I just say goodbye and die instead.
  3. Seems too bad to me that handling bodily waste products (shit and urine) is such a difficult and repulsive task but that is how things are. If I am in a condition that I cannot deal with such waste (especially shit) in approximately the "normal" way I have known for most of my life--sitting on a toilet, pooping into the toilet, then cleaning up with toilet paper or a bidet--I don't want to continue to live.
  4. If I am bed-bound, unable to move around in the world with just the assistance of a high-end wheelchair, I think that will be a deal breaker and it will be time to go. I can imagine a situation where I am lucid, present, and thinking clearly but still unable to move around much that might be a mitigating circumstance--but it seems like a long shot so I vote for the end rather than wonder.
  5. Lastly, to help with the difficult decision that may well come up, let me provide a rule to be applied that should, hopefully, make it a bit easier. There will likely be a huge grey area between Death and Life--if, when weighing the circumstances for and against dying, the balance comes out to Life without at least a 20% margin, I want to die. To put it another way, if the answer comes up "maybe" please erase that answer and replace it with "it's time to let him go".

I am so sorry to have to put you, my beloved family, through this ordeal but this seemed like the least painful way to do it. I truly hope none of this will matter in the end. Perhaps I will be lucky enough to just die quietly in my sleep. That is my deepest hope. But just in case that fantasy doesn't come true, thank you so much for dealing with this most painful decision. I hope I have helped with this note.



Thomas Brennan-Marquez
16 Apr 2024

BEST Tinnitus Relief Sound Therapy Treatment | 10 Hours of Tinnitus Masking


https://youtube.com/watch?v=s3jYDf3MjoU

    $60.00Add to cart
    

    Revolutionize Your Laser Engraving with Da Big Gimping Plugin V4: The Premier Photo Processing Solution
    

    Transform your laser engraving projects with ease using the Da Big Gimping Plugin V4. Designed for both beginners and seasoned engravers, this plugin automates your photo editing, saving you time and enhancing the quality of your engravings.
    

    Effortless Windows Installation with Auto Installer
    

      

    1. Download the Auto Installer: Securely download the Da Big Gimping Plugin V4 Auto Installer.
      2. 

    2. Prepare for Installation: Ensure all previous versions of the plugin are uninstalled. Download and extract the installer to your ‘Downloads’ folder.
      3. 

    3. Run the Auto Installer: Close Gimp, execute the installer file, and follow the on-screen instructions.
      4. 

    4. Restart Gimp: Launch Gimp again to discover the plugin ready for use in the top menu.
      5. 

    

    Note: The Auto Installer simplifies the setup process, eliminating manual file copying.
    

    Setting Permissions on Mac OS
    

    (No changes, as this section remains relevant as is.)
    

    Seamless Workflow from Gimp to Lightburn
    

     
    

    Photo Engraving Workflow Chart – Gimp To Lightburn
    

      

    1. Install the Gimp software on your computer.
      2. 

    2. Download the Da Big Gimping V4 plugin file and save it to your computer.
      3. 

    3. Open Gimp and go to Edit > Preferences > Folders > Plug-ins.
      4. 

    4. Click on the “New” button and navigate to the folder where you saved the plugin file.
      5. 

    5. Select the plugin file and click on “Open”.
      6. 

    6. Close and re-open Gimp to apply the changes.
      7. 

    7. Open a photo in Gimp in RGB mode. Do not press OK yet.
      8. 

    8. We’re going to convert the photo to inches to mm. Take note of the final image size.
      9. 

    9. Prepare to run the V4 plugin.
      10. 

    10. Run the V4 plugin by selecting it from the “Filters” or “Tools” menu depending on its functionality.
      11. 

    11. Press OK and wait for the automated processing to finish.
      12. 

    12. Save the processed photo using the “File > Export As” menu.</
      13. 

    

    

    Da Big Gimping Plugin V4: A Game Changer in Photo Engraving
    

    Step into the future of photo processing for laser engraving with Da Big Gimping Plugin V4. This innovative tool is a must-have for laser engraving enthusiasts, offering unparalleled ease of use and quality enhancements. Whether you’re a hobbyist or a professional, the Da Big Gimping Plugin V4 is your key to unlocking stunning laser engravings with minimal effort.

    Exciting News: Da Big Gimping Plugin V4 – Now with Windows Auto Installer!
    

     Latest Update: January 3rd, 2024
    
We’re thrilled to announce a major update to Da Big Gimping Plugin V4 – now featuring a seamless Windows auto-installer! Making your photo processing journey smoother and more efficient than ever.
    

     Previous Updates:
    

      

    • October 1st, 2022: Introduced a nifty dither option.
      • 

    • August 14th, 2022: Teased the upcoming release of Gimp V4, complete with a user-friendly auto installer.
      • 

    • September 14th, 2022: Official release of Da Big Gimping Plug-in V4, along with the Windows auto installer.
      • 

    

    

    Da Big Gimping V4 Plug-in: A Revolution in Photo Processing
    

    Today marks a milestone with the release of V4 – an automated photo processing tool that transforms your workflow.
    

    ️ Installation Simplified: Manual & Automatic
    

    Automatic Installation on Windows:
    

      

    • Step 1: Ensure previous versions are uninstalled – delete old zip files and their extracted folders.
      • 

    • Step 2: Download the new version to your ‘Downloads’ folder and extract it there.
      • 

    • Step 3: Close GIMP, run the installer file, reopen GIMP, and voila! You’re all set to explore the new features.
      • 

    

    Coming Soon: Installation Tutorial Video!
    

    ️ User Interface: Intuitive and Powerful
    

    Experience simplicity and power with the Da Big Gimping Plugin V4 UI:
    

      

    1. Open & Select: Begin with any photo of your choice.
      2. 

    2. Choose Your Process: Opt for the lens-based process (1.5″ lens recommended for lifelike results).
      3. 

    3. DPI Settings: Set a minimum of 400 DPI, but don’t hesitate to explore higher settings.
      4. 

    4. Image Sizing: Customize your image size in millimeters.
      5. 

    5. Process & Save: Hit OK, let the automated magic happen, and save your image. We recommend TIFF for quality, or JPEG at 100% quality.
      6. 

    6. Laser Ready: Import the processed photo to your laser software. For Lightburn users, enable ‘Pass Thru Mode’; for Rdworks, select ‘Output Direct’.
      7. 

    

     Perfect Your Craft
    

    With Da Big Gimping V4, your photos will not just be processed; they’ll be transformed. Get ready for a game-changing experience in photo processing for laser engraving. Your journey to stunning, laser-sharp images starts here!
    

    

    Keep an eye out for more updates and tips to get the most out of Da Big Gimping Plugin V4! 
    

      

      2. 

    

     
    

     
    

    

    The Results Are In!

    Da Big Gimping Plugin v3 Photo Process Is Here – Gimp – Da big gimpin
    

    I’ve been working on developing an awesome automated photo process that works with gimp and its finally ready to go and I call it da big gimping plugin
    

    I have done tests on painted glass, painted acrylic, painted tile and wood while calibrating this laser photo engraving process, I will include a small gallery below for you to see how well this photo process for GIMP works for laser engraving photos. Da big gimpin plugin
    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    

    


    

    

    Da big gimpin plugin – Da big gimpin plug-in
    

    Da big gimping plug-in is now used vastly throughout the laser engraving community.

		g.oxford 
		(verified owner) 		– April 6, 2020
	
      

	
      Thanks Sean… I’ve been trying to figure out how to process photos myself for laser engraving for the last 3 or 4 months.  I’ve watched peoples videos, read tutorials, tried to replicate their results in GIMP without success.  I’ve tried the 1Touch Laser Photo software and did have decent results on wood with that but not great results on painted tile.  Finally got around to buying this script and WOW, it’s blowing me away.  It’s so much easier to just have to figure out speed and power settings for each material than it was trying to process the photo on my own first.  This product is well worth the cost, especially when the time and material wasted trying to figure out processing myself is factored in.

    .                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
      
                               

      Description 
      
    

        
    Thinking about making your jigs and lures colorful? Try on this Pro-Tec Powder Paint and have the most attractive fish baits that will definitely get those fish biting onto them. This powder coating paint has virtually no odor and no solvents and it leaves a high gloss finishing on your tackle box fishing accessories in just a few seconds. It allows you to save time as it doesn't require mixing and drying time and it's very easy to clean. You'll also save extra bucks for you don't need a gloss coat or primer coat. Each 2-ounce jar of this premium-quality and high-gloss Pro-Tec Powder Paint can cover 3,000 of 3/8oz jigs. It helps increase the glow properties of any fishing jigs or fishing lures within less than 20 minutes of painting. DIRECTIONS: Shake briefly to fluff paint. Preheat metal lures using a small torch, oven, or any flame for 4-10 seconds depending on size. Quickly swish into powder and remove, knocking off excess. The lure should be hot enough to melt powder within 2-4 seconds after removal. Let cool. For maximum durability, hang and bake in a preheated 350°F (176°C) oven for 20 minutes. Always pop out the eye before curing. IMPORTANT REMINDERS: 1.) After swishing several jigs, the powder will settle. Shake or stir to keep loose and fluffy. Call for production methods using powder. 2.) Store in a cool area. INCLUDES: 1 x 2oz Pro-Tec Powder Paint for Lures and Jigs
       
         
            
      
                                                                                     
      
                               
             Features & details   
            
                   
        
               
    • A VERY PRACTICAL CHOICE OF PAINT – Pro-Tec Powder Paint completes the process of painting in less than 20 minutes. It's known for its durable paint and high gloss.
      •   
    • A VERY PRACTICAL CHOICE OF PAINT – Pro-Tec Powder Paint completes the process of painting in less than 20 minutes. It's known for its durable paint and high gloss.
      •   
    • A VERY PRACTICAL CHOICE OF PAINT – Pro-Tec Powder Paint completes the process of painting in less than 20 minutes. It's known for its durable paint and high gloss.
      •            
    • A VERY PRACTICAL CHOICE OF PAINT – Pro-Tec Powder Paint completes the process of painting in less than 20 minutes. It's known for its durable paint and high gloss.
      •   
    • A VERY PRACTICAL CHOICE OF PAINT – Pro-Tec Powder Paint completes the process of painting in less than 20 minutes. It's known for its durable paint and high gloss.
      •   
    • VIBRANT COLOR – Helps increase the glow properties of fishing lures and fishing jigs and leaves a very attractive color with a super high gloss that will invite a lot of fish to catch.
      •   
    • SAVE TIME AND MONEY – One-coat coverage with no white primer needed. No mixing and no drying time needed. Easy clean-up.
      •   
    • ENVIRONMENTALLY-SAFE AND NON-FLAMMABLE - A powder coating paint with no heavy odor and does not contain any solvents.
      •   
    • PAINT MORE JIGS AND LURES – Each 2-ounce jar can cover 3,000 of 3/8oz jigs. A very practical choice for anglers, fishermen, and tackle manufacturers.
      •

Statements regarding dietary supplements have not been evaluated by the FDA, and these products are not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease or condition.

      Vocabulary Words 
    
          
    Vocabulary Words


    Word
    Date Entered Here
    Definition
    synecdoche
    [Thu 6-Dec-18 12:01 PM]
    a figure of speech by which a part is put for the whole (such as fifty sail for fifty ships), the whole for a part (such as society for high society), the species for the genus (such as cutthroat for assassin), the genus for the species (such as a creature for a man), or the name of the material for the thing made (such as boards for stage)
    synectics
    [Thu 6-Dec-18 12:02 PM]

    the study of creative processes, especially as applied to the solution of problems by a group of diverseindividuals
    Urolagnia (also urophilia, and, more colloquially, a golden shower
    Coprophagia (/ˌkɒprəˈfeɪdʒiə/ KOP-rə-FAY-jee-ə) or coprophagy (/kəˈprɒfədʒi/ kə-PROF-ə-jee) is the consumption of feces.

      Principal Sunday Holy Eucharist - Washington National Cathedral 
    
          
    Web Clip

    Principal Sunday Holy Eucharist
    
												
    
											
    
				
    
			
    
		
    
		
					
    
									
    
						
    
							
							May 12, 2024 - 11:15am
						
    
					
    
								
				
				
									
    
						
    
							
															Nave
													
    

											
    
				
									
    
						
    
							
							Free
						
    
					
    
							
    
			
    

    

	
    
		

		
		
    
			
    
				
    
					
					
    
						
    
																					
    
    Our principal weekly Holy Eucharist anchors our worship life and prepares us for the week ahead. The Cathedral’s soaring architecture will point your eyes toward heaven as prayers, preaching and music open our hearts to God. Our worship is based on the traditions of The Episcopal Church and features beloved hymns, a hybrid blend of traditional and contemporary music and the best in Cathedral preaching. All who seek a deeper relationship with God in Christ are welcome to receive Communion.

    

    
    Seventh Sunday of Easter

    

    
    Presider: The Rev. Canon Dana Colley Corsello
    
    
    Preacher: The Rev. Canon Jan Naylor Cope

    

    
    Join us in-person, or watch the livestream
    
        on our Holy Eucharist page
    
    or on the Cathedral’s
    
        YouTube channel.
    
    ASL interpretation is offered for this service.

    

    
    Learn more about Worship at the Cathedral:
    
        Frequently Asked Questions

    Chesapeake and Ohio Canal Trail and River Trail Loop 
          
          
      
      
      
        
      
      
    
    
      John is Dead 
    
          
    just finished reading T's three pages of musings about John, the drugs, the betrayal, the missed chances. i had to write something as well because that sting (the sonofabitch!) while buried deep is just a scabbed over scar.
    many times i have thought that John made such a sad choice when he elected to live in the drugged world when that would of course mean that he lived out of the loving relationship world that was there--but disintegrating fast by the end. back in berkeley he had people who cared about him. and not just some knuckleheads who were just scraping by, but some really high quality people that could have helped him (as he was just scraping by so much of the time). but no, he traded that for the escape, the good feeling that the drugs could provide.
    T and I were just talking about the phrase "he did the best he could". that phrase evokes two strong feelings in me that apply quite clearly to John: 
    1. yes, i know you could not handle things any other way because you were so broken emotionally and handicapped genetically... 
    2. but you didn't even try to do the work it would take to dig yourself out of that self-pitying hole--you just took the easy way (although that path would prove to be less than an easy ride) and took no responsibility for any of it
    several times i considered (very briefly) making contact with him to try to bridge the chasm that had formed when he disappeared into alcohol and cocaine. the idea was fleeting because (just as Terese has described in her pages) nothing really had changed. there would be lies. there would be excuses. there would be excellent, unavoidable reasons why everything in his life happened just the way it did. it didn't take much consideration to conclude that such a meeting would only lead to more pain and frustration.
    so, goodbye, John. we were the best of friends once--and then you disappeared into a cravasse so deep i would literally never see you again. 
    and now i wonder: what feelings did it bring up for you when you thought back to those nights we played our guitars together, writing music that wasn't very good but was somewhat cathartic? how did you remember the months we lived in Seattle, somehow playing at living because we didn't really know how yet to be real people? and, in the end, when you drove away from our townhouse, did you know that you would never see any of us again? did that break your heart or did that just seem like the way it had to go--because you were always the victim of circumstance and none of it was ever really your fault.

      Notes on generating XSD 
    
          
    • each class gets a plural container:
    <xs:element name="RangeLimitations">
                    <xs:complexType>
                            <xs:sequence>
                                    <xs:element maxOccurs="unbounded" ref="RangeLimitation"/>
                            </xs:sequence>
                    </xs:complexType>
            </xs:element>
    • each class gets an element:
    <xs:element name="EngineeringUnit">
                    <xs:complexType>
                            <xs:attribute name="Description" use="required"/>
                            <xs:attribute name="Name" use="required" type="xs:ID"/>
                    </xs:complexType>
            </xs:element>
    • a class gets formalization attributes, indicating conditionality this way:
    <xs:element name="DataTopic">
                    <xs:complexType>
                            <xs:sequence>                        note: use all rather than sequence to allow ordering
                                    <xs:element minOccurs="1" ref="StructuredData"/>
                                    <xs:element minOccurs="0" ref="RangeLimitation"/>
                            </xs:sequence>
                            <xs:attribute name="DeprecatedVersion" use="required"/>
                            <xs:attribute name="Description" use="required"/>
                            <xs:attribute name="IntroducedVersion" use="required" type="xs:NMTOKEN"/>
                            <xs:attribute name="Name" use="required" type="xs:NCName"/>
                    </xs:complexType>
            </xs:element>
    • each simple attribute of a class (which must be set to something!) gets:
    <xs:element name="RangeLimitation">
                    <xs:complexType>
                            <xs:attribute name="IsUpperLimit" type="bool"/>
                            <xs:attribute name="LimitingValue" use="required" type="xs:integer"/>
                            <xs:attribute name="Name" use="required" type="xs:NCName"/>
                            <xs:attribute name="Color" type="color"  use="required" />           
                    </xs:complexType>
            </xs:element>
    • two ways to refer to an instance (first one is embedded define of a new instance element, second one is a formal attribute pointing to an instance elsewhere:
    <DataTopic Name="RaControlDesired" IntroducedVersion="D" DeprecatedVersion="4"                      Description="description of RaControlDesired ">
    			<StructuredData Name="RaControlDesired_SD"/>
    			<RangeLimitation LimitingValue="100" Name="myveryownlimitation" Color="red"/>
    		</DataTopic>
    		<DataTopic Name="UiActual" IntroducedVersion="E" DeprecatedVersion="5" Description="description of UiActual "    RangeLimitation="BozoRange">
    			<StructuredData Name="UiActual_SD"/>
    		</DataTopic>
    • instance created as children of an element may be referenced by other elements outside the parent element--scoping rules (ala C++) don't apply

      Letter of Recommendation (Nathan Gong) 
    
          
    I am writing to recommend Nathan Gong for admission to the graduate program in Computer Science at Columbia University, Fu Foundation School of Engineering. I have known Nathan for about a year during his employment at Medtronic, Inc. He and I worked together on a project to apply a new data handling system to our robotic surgery system.
    Nathan proved to be a quick study. When we started on this project he had no previous experience the new Troika data handling system that was under development in my group. I supervised Nathan's work, attacking a problem that needed solving by our GUI group and to which we wanted to apply the Troika technology. He needed very little in the way of instruction;I just explained the goal and pointed him to the Troika tools he would use. He was quite willing to ask questions and for clarification a few times. But overall, he went off to work by himself to produce a first draft of a solution. We discussed his work, made a few easy improvements, then he took his work to the GUI team. That team was pleased with what he had produced (and surprised at how quickly he had accomplished it). Nathan worked with the GUI group very comfortably then, bringing  his solution into alignment with exactly what they wanted.  
    Nathan is bright and eager to learn new things. He was a pleasure to work with and I believe he would be an excellent student in your program. I give him my highest recommendation.
    If I can provide any further assistance, do not hesitate to contact me:
    650.815.1056
    thomas.r.brennan-marquez@medtronic.com

    Thomas Brennan-Marquez 
          
          
      
      
      
        
        
        
      
      
    
    
      How to Microdose - Getting Started | Microdosing Institute 
    
          
    Web Clip
    


     
    Home » Microdosing Guide—Your Microdosing Roadmap » Getting Started—How to Microdose
    Getting Started—How to Microdose
    After learning about the benefits of microdosing, researching the subject thoroughly, and deciding on the microdosing substance and protocol, it’s time to get hands-on! Continue reading for our practical guidelines on how to microdose safely, consciously, and effectively. 
    If you feel that you’d like to learn more about the risks, safety, substances or just want to find out if microdosing might be the right pathway for you—have a look at our 2.5-hour video course EXPLORE MICRODOSING.
    In both the microdosing video course and the practical guide below we combined our extensive knowledge with the experiences of our 6000+ member community, scientific findings, and thousands of anecdotal reports from microdosers all over the world.  
    First things first: When is microdosing not recommended?
    • When you are younger than 18 years old
    • When you are pregnant or breastfeeding
    • In case of a mental or physical condition and/or illness
    • If you have color blindness
    • In extreme cases of situations in your life that are beyond your control (for instance: grief)
    • In combination with alcohol or other drugs
    • In combination with lithium carbonate
    • In combination with Tramadol
    • In combination with lithium carbonate
    • When in the process of tapering off from antidepressants or anti-anxiety medication
    These ‘How to microdose’ guidelines follow the Fadiman Protocol, but you can adjust these according to your desired protocol. The Fadiman Protocol (also known as the Beginners Protocol) is the most well-known microdosing schedule named after Dr. James Fadiman. The primary aim is to help microdosers observe the effects of microdosing on their lives by clearly distinguishing between microdosing days and non-microdosing days. The Fadiman Protocol is an excellent basis for a first microdosing experiment. It assumes a 3-day cycle, which you follow for four to eight weeks.
    MICRODOSING PROTOCOL: 
    • DAY 1: 1st microdosing day
    • DAY 2: transition day
    • DAY 3: normal day
    • DAY 4: 2nd microdosing day
    CYCLE: Continue this cycle for four to eight weeks
    RESET: Two to four weeks rest
     
    Step 1—preparing the microdose
    One of the most important steps in learning how to microdose: accurately measuring the dose. We recommend to microdose with dried or liquid substances instead of fresh products, because it’s easier to maintain a consistent potency with each dose. Because the sale of sacred earth medicines such as psilocybin mushrooms or magic truffles, and San Pedro cactus are illegal in most countries, you may have to find a reliable underground source* or cultivate them yourself for personal use.
    When measuring your first microdose, make sure to use an electric scale that measures up to two decimals. We recommend starting with the smallest dose and gradually increasing the dose each microdosing day if you feel you need more to find your “sweet spot”. 
    In the Netherlands, it’s possible to purchase fresh ready-to-use microdosing truffles that are vacuum-sealed in portions of 6 ×1 gram. There is no need for a scale, capsules, or even drying the truffles. Simply store them in the fridge and take between 0.5 and 2 portions (depending on your sweet spot).
    Although iboga root bark/tincture is sometimes microdosed, we only share this information for educational purposes. Because iboga has such different properties and variety of direct physical effects, we don’t recommend microdosing with this substance.
    Recommended doses for microdosing for each substance
    • Dried magic truffles: 0.2–1.0 gram
    • Fresh microdosing truffles: 0.5–2.0 gram (0,5–2 portions of XP Truffles)
    • Dried magic mushrooms: 0.1–0.3 gram
    • Dried San Pedro cactus: 5.0–1.5 gram
    • LSD: 5.0–15.0 microgram (important: apply volumetric dosing to obtain this level of precision)
    • 1cP-LSD or 1P-LSD: 5.0–15.0 microgram
    • Dried iboga: 0.3–0.5 gram
    • LSA: 0.1–0.5 milligram (1/3–1 Hawaiian baby wood rose seed or 5-15 morning glory seeds
    • Ayahuasca: please refer to the B. Caapi vine page
    NOTE: Microdosing with fresh magic mushrooms, iboga, DMT, and other psychedelic substances is uncommon, or best dosing practices haven’t been established yet. Therefore, we don’t mentioned no doses on this platform.
       
    Fresh XP-Truffles (6x1gram for €6,-) available at Microdose.nl
    Step 2—taking the first microdose
    DAY 1: The first microdosing day
    We strongly advise to getting started with microdosing on a day without any obligations (work, family, social). It’s best to take your microdose before 10AM in the morning on an empty stomach. This way, it will take effect much quicker. Many people experience an increased energy level, so it’s advisable to start in the morning and enjoy a more energetic day. Should you decide to take your microdose in the middle of the day, you might have some difficulties falling asleep when it’s time for bed.
    What if the microdose is too strong?
    If the dose makes you feel as if it’s somewhat too strong, you may experience for instance a lack of focus, some confusion, restlessness, or a headache. Please know that you are physically safe at all times, and you won’t “trip” from the small dose that you’ve taken. However, here are some tips from our team to navigate the experience (better):
    • Surrender. Don’t try to resist the experience, this causes unpleasant friction.
    • Do some breathing exercises (e.g., the 4-7-8 breath exercise) 
    • Relax and meditate
    • Take a walk outside
    • Listen to your favorite music
    • Make a herbal tea for a calming effect (valerian herb works great)
    NOTE: If the dose feels too weak, don’t top up with an extra dose, but increase your dose a little on the next microdosing day.
    What to do in case of an unpleasant physical reaction?
    If you experience an unpleasant physical reaction to microdosing, such as headache, nausea, or fatigue, it’s essential that you investigate this.
    • First, determine if this is purely a physical reaction to the substance
      • Your dose may be too high
      • Another substance may be better for you
    • Take the time and space to connect with the feeling. Without judgment, focus on it and sit with it. Often there is another cause than directly related to the dose or substance; microdosing has a persistent tendency to point out areas where you need to do some (inner). 
    • What is this sensation trying to tell you? What does it require from you? This self-examination is worthwhile because there can be many causes for abdominal pain, headaches, or other physical reactions.
    In either case, you’ll learn to become more aware of yourself, of what lives within you, and how to take action. This awareness process allows you to create a life in which you gradually become more authentic. This process is the foundation and ultimate goal of microdosing!
    The chance is very slim, however…
    • If you experience a lot of discomfort (for example, an allergic reaction) or your physical health is in danger, stop microdosing immediately. The reaction will go away on its own. 
    • If necessary, contact your doctor or seek medical care. Know that they have a duty of confidentiality, so you’ll never be judged or sentenced here for using a non-legal substance.
    • Please also inform us—this way we can better educate future microdosers. 
     Learn How To Microdose 
     This hands-on video course guarantees you don't miss or misinterpret any important aspect of microdosing. 
     From preparation, dosing and calibration to result tracking, optimizing your benefits, and applying your findings and insights into your daily life — we've got you covered!
     Be guided on ever step of your own unique microdosing journey. 
      Access the Video Course 
    Step 3—keeping a (microdosing) journal
    Especially during your first microdosing experiment, daily journaling about your observations, feelings, and insights is invaluable from the first day of microdosing (even if only a few keywords). By checking in with yourself on a daily basis and taking short notes, you can quickly see what changes are taking place. Your collected insights are more important than you might initially realize. Overall, journaling helps you make your observations more concrete and tangible and translate them into clear action points for your life.
     
    Below is a list of topics and journal questions that have proven effective for many people in our community. You can adopt these and possibly add other habits or qualities that you would like to track. At the end of each day (transition and normal days included!) rate these variables on a scale from 1 to 10. Then take notes of what else you have noticed and the insights you have gained.
    Daily self-assessment:
    *These ratings can be reversed (for example, less stress = higher rating).
    Mood
    • Awareness of positive emotions
    • Awareness of (and dealing with) negative emotions*
    • Relationships and social interactions
    • Self-love and taking care of yourself
    • Stress*
    Physical
    • Energy level
    • Sleep
    • Sport and exercise
    • Nutrition and eating habits
    • Pain*
    Work & study
    • Focus
    • Productivity
    • Creativity
    • Flow
    Daily journal questions:
    • What have you noticed, in the positive sense, today?
    • What thoughts, feelings and emotions did you perceive in yourself?
    • Did something occur to you today, internally or in your actions, that was different from what you would normally expect?
    Reflecting repeatedly on these areas makes you even more aware of this inner journey. So step into the role of the observer for a moment. Make sure you take notes of the insights you gain along the way, such as exciting thoughts and inspirations. And if you feel the need to continue writing, do so! That helps to integrate all your insights into more concrete ways to improve your life.
    Step 4—creating subtle awareness
    DAY 2 : Transition day
    On the day after a microdosing you don’t take a dose—this is called a transition day. These days are just as crucial as microdosing days! The microdosing substance will be present in the brain and body for two days. Be conscious on these days about what you are feeling, experiencing, and thinking, but try not to judge or overthink. Instead, briefly write in your journal about the subtle differences that you notice. Putting your reflections on paper gives your subconscious mind an opportunity to speak.  
     
    Step 5—resetting the body
    DAY 3: Normal day
    Day 3 is also a non-microdosing day and is considered a normal day because the substance is not active in your system anymore. By building in normal days into your microdosing routine, you avoid tolerance of the substance (requiring more each time to achieve the same effect) and create clear distinctions between the microdosing and transition days.  
    Step 6—finding the sweet spot
    DAY 4: Second microdosing day
    On day four, you take the second microdose. Consider taking more, less, or the same amount of the substance as on the first day. The most important step when working with microdosing is determining your ideal dose, called the “sweet spot”. The rule is, you should be able to function normally during your daily activities and social interactions while microdosing. When you feel a slight presence of the substance but not so much that it interferes with your daily routine, you’ve found your ideal dose or “sweet spot”.
    Dr. James Fadiman talks about a “sub-perceptual” dosage, which means there should be no visual disturbances. However, this doesn’t mean that you shouldn’t notice any difference at all! You can certainly feel the effect, although not overpowering in a way that it would require too much direct focus (instead of directing your attention to your daily activities).   
    The goal is to find the ideal dose within the first four times of microdosing. If you want to experience the power of your substance and get a good sense of what it ‘feels like, you can choose to start with the highest microdose (as indicated in the dosing chart) on a day off. From there, you can gradually decrease the dose until you’ve found your sweet spot.
    Here are some indicators found your sweet spot:
    • During your day, you forgot that you took a microdose
    • You feel as if you’re looking through the magnifying glass: you have a sense of wonder and effortlessness as you go about your day
    • At the end of your day, you reflect and think: ‘This was a really good day’.
    In any case, take your time to become aware of the presence of the substance in your system. We recommend taking a nice walk in nature on your first microdosing day to connect with yourself. Until you’ve found your sweet spot, it’s advisable not to drive a car or use other motorized devices!
     
    Step 7—creating balance
    DAY 4–30
    Microdosing is a powerful tool, but we don’t recommend microdosing every day. In this case, “less is more”. LSD and psilocybin remain active in the body for about 48 hours. If you microdose on a daily basis, your body develops tolerance and microdosing will become a habit in the same way that drinking coffee can become a habit.
    This way, you won’t be able to accurately monitor how the substance affects your behavior such as productivity, creativity, and mood. According to Dr. James Fadiman’s data, the tolerance shouldn’t occur if you adhere to any of the known microdosing protocols.
    To prevent psychological habituation, he also advises taking a break of at least a month after each microdosing cycle. This is also done for safety reasons because there has been no research into the long-term effects and risks of microdosing. Read more about the risks of microdosing.
    For more guidance at any stage of your microdosing journey, go to our guidance page—we offer 1:1 guidance, coaching, a community-founded online course, and more. 
    Disclaimer
    The content on this website and the other Microdosing Institute channels is for informational and educational purposes only, and does not substitute professional medical advice or consultation with healthcare professionals. If you are seeking medical advice, diagnose, or treatment, we advise you to consult a medical professional or healthcare provider. Equally, we cannot help with the sourcing of illegal substances.
    


          
          
      
      
      
        
      
      
    
    
      Hugo Projects 
    
          
    • XML Format
      • Convert to the XML representation of "tab" files
      • Generate XSD from EA model to encode the schema and guide developer's in VSCode
    •  SWI Tagging
      • Build a scanner to find tags in files (done)
      • Capture all those tags/source file associations in Rosetta database
      • Build a scanner to identify blame devs to know who to ask "what SWI is this?" for those that are not yet tagged
      • combine three features from Jan into just one of them
        • - http://jira.covidien.com:8080/browse/TAP-112245  [FSTR][Comms][Cleanup] Include SWI tags in generated code
        • - http://jira.covidien.com:8080/browse/TAP-112047  [FSTR][cleanup] Complete SWI Tagging
        • - http://jira.covidien.com:8080/browse/TAP-112046  [FSTR][cleanup] Each file contains only one SWI
    • Capturing Data
      • Move all of Marieluisa's SWI/DataTopic associations into Rosetta format
    • Dmytro's Generated File
      • iron out the last few details of the gen'd file
    • Preparing for PI Planning
      • combine all 3 features into a single feature
      • construct user stories to detail the work to be done
        • build a scanner to find
          
          
      
      
      
        
      
      
    
    
      T-Mobile Overcharging 
    
          
    _________  Sat 20-Apr-24 11:45 AM _________

    • on the phone with them for at least 1:48
    • talked to three levels of people
      • first one said "something is very wrong here"


      • latest one (i am waiting to speak to her supervisor) said they can only return 3 months worth of charges
    • we had two lines with them (maybe from when i signed up in Santa Clara?)
    • canceled those on may 21, 2023
      • cost was $120/month but jumped to $130/month after cancellation
      • they claimed this number 650.278.7689 was still active
        1. two possibilities here:
          • this might be the Google number alias I got many years ago
            • seems kinda familiar but i don't remember for sure
            • i certainly didn't tell T-Mobile about that number from Google
          • that sign-up in Santa Clara included, for some reason, another "free" line that i didn't ever activate--i think i have something on paper in a file somewhere that would have that free number written down
        2. either way, there was never a phone device associated with the number
        3. there will be no record of traffic on that number either
        4. oddly, they didn't send any emails to say thanks for the payment
          • last one of those in my archive is dated Mar 21 2023 (just two months before cancellation date) and it says $120
          • no later notices of charges
        5. checking ...1914 card bank statements
          • they have been charging $130 every month since cancellation
        6. found this when i was categorizing charges on Simplifi

      PAX PLUS Dry Herb & Concentrate Vaporizer | PAX 
    
          

    PAX PLUS Dry Herb & Concentrate Vaporizer | PAX
    Web Clip
